  1| # Checkpoint Scenarios
  2| 
  3| This document describes how the one-to-one checkpoint system handles various user workflows.
  4| 
  5| ## Overview
  6| 
  7| The system uses:
  8| - **Shadow branches** (`entire/<commit-hash>-<worktree-hash>`) - temporary storage for checkpoint data
  9| - **FilesTouched** - accumulates files modified during the session

docs/architecture/attribution.md:1-80
  1| # Attribution
  2| 
  3| ## Overview
  4| 
  5| Attribution tracks how much of a commit came from the agent vs the user. When a user commits code after working with an AI agent, the CLI calculates what percentage of the changes were agent-written vs human-written.
  6| 
  7| This is captured in the `Entire-Attribution` trailer on commits:
  8| 
  9| ```
  10| feat: Add user authentication
  11| 
  12| Entire-Checkpoint: a3b2c4d5e6f7
  13| Entire-Attribu
